channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Trees have not been pruned since 2008 -now massive, 11th April
item.description:
Trees now overhang the complete garden and property, breaking roof tiles, blocking out light (when in leaf) and causing damage - trees need to be brought under control and have the canopy much reduced in size.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 84, All Saints' Road, Fairview, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Collapsed fencing onto highway, 11th April
item.description:
Fencing panels next to 9a Millbrook have. come away from posts. It protects people from accessing the bank of the river Chelt. I think the fencing is public not linked to a garden. Panel falling onto highway.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14, Millbrook Street, Alstone, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Other damage to pavement i.e. cobbles, raised mound/surface

item.title: Flytipped item, 11th April
item.description:
Tub of liquid near 16 Dunalley Parade

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5 Courtney Villas, Dunalley Parade, Marle Hill, Cheltenham

item.title: Flytipped item, 11th April
item.description:
Small tyre next to the Hindu Centre

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Milsom Street, Marle Hill, Cheltenham

item.title: Fly tipping, 11th April
item.description:
This rubbish appeared overnight. THIS IS THE THIRD TIME RUBBISH HAS BEEN DUMPED HERE SINCE TUESDAY. Last week we had 4 seperate dumps.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 66, Pates Avenue, Alstone, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: 18" pothole in merestones drive, 11th April
item.description:
In middle of road
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4, Merestones Drive, Warden Hill, Cheltenham

item.title: Flytipped items, 11th April
item.description:
At the corner of Winterbotham Road lots of random rubbish

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Oldbury Road, Kingsditch, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: American flags hanging from lamp posts, 11th April
item.description:
Please remove the American flags (and other flags for that matter) from the lamp posts. They have been there for nearly 2 months. I presume it is not now legal for anyone to hang whatever flags they want from flag posts.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 122, Station Road, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rotting knee rails - falling to bits, 10th April
item.description:
Repair all the rotting knee rails in the area of the photos which have been rotting for some time now, they are falling bits and looking terrible. Have been informed that all of the knee rails identified in these areas fall under the responsibility of Tewksbury Borough Council as they adopted the open spaces in these areas in 2025.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tiger Moth Close, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Wooden knee rails rotting and falling to bits, 10th April
item.description:
Repair all the rotting knee rails in the area of the photos which have been rotting for some time now, they are falling bits and looking terrible. Have been informed that all of the knee rails identified in these areas fall under the responsibility of Tewksbury Borough Council as they adopted the open spaces in these areas in 2025.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Guan Road, Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drain cover is rattling again!!! The drain is also full and needs to emptied, 10th April
item.description:
Outside Lorien This is being regularly fixed can a proper permanent repair be done.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Stoke Road, Stoke Orchard, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Part of this massive pothole os 50cm wide and 7cm deep., 10th April
item.description:
Near to Hillsdown and Watermead Farm in the lane which is a single track no pavements. Pothole is usually filled with rain water but has now dried out due to weather to show how deep it is. Very dangerous to vehicle suspension when it is wet - due to no visibility of the depth of this pothole which is usually filled with rain water, and could be a potential trip hazard to pedestrians.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brockworth, Tewkesbury
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Double yellows worn off, 10th April
item.description:
Double yellows worn and almost invisible outside premier Regular dangerous parking, repainting will hopefully discourage this
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 1, Merriville Road, Wymans Brook, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Road marking white lines or studs

item.title: The potholes in beeches road (opposite no 7) and at junction with little herberts road, 10th April
item.description:
These potholes have now become dangerously deep and almost unavoidable. Danger of collision with oncoming traffic. Last reported over a month ago and still awaiting attention.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 5, Beeches Road, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish and overgrown brambles etc and now vermin, 10th April
item.description:
The area behind Welch road and back of lipson road the ex garages site is filled with fly tipping and so overgrown that there are now rats coming into my garden this is an ongoing problem that is only going to get worse with summer coming

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 24, Lipson Road, Kingsditch, Cheltenham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree is damaged problem, 10th April
item.description:
Email received 08/04/2026 - copy attached. Customer reports that the Lime Tree at the location has caused the boundary wall to subside and consequently it has been taken down. The wall needs to be rebuilt but they are wary of doing so, as the tree could potentially cause the same issues for this as well.
Report on FixMyStreet
